entry.title: Quedada SAP - Let's talk about Joule - Lima 🇵🇪 #SAPCodeJamsLATAM
entry.content:
The local SAP Community in Lima has a chance to meet after our first of two local SAP CodeJams!

Quedada SAP in Lima will happen on August 4th, 2024 at 18:00 local time for a few hours, so join us and be a part of our Group!  You don't have to be an attendee of our SAP CodeJam events, just someone active or curious about the SAP Community and our focus on #developers

Let’s talk about what's new in the SAP world, about interesting SAP projects in the region, and what's new with ourselves.

Please let us know if you will participate in SAP Stammtisch by clicking "I will attend", at Bar Aleman Miraflores, Av. Alfredo Benavides 511, Lima 15074, Peru.  FYI, everyone is responsible for their own to buy a food/drink item if they wish, we are just happy for the chance to connect in person and have some relaxing conversation!

Click here to find more information about the SAP Developer Advocates second Latin America Roadshow

entry.title: Re: How to Mass Maintain WBS Element for Multiple PO Item...
entry.content: Isn't the WBS field available in App ID F2593 - Mass Changes to Purchase Orders?

entry.title: Re: Hi there, I want revoke very same question. Has anyone has solution?
entry.content: I believe that, this problem is related with permission for Manage Center of Capabilities. When I enter this action, I see only white screen. Anyone has any idea, in which situation this problem occured?

entry.title: Re: S_ALR_87012394 can be used with Fiori
entry.content:
Use the SAP Fiori Apps Reference Library to find/identify available Apps.

  • Prepare Tax Report, Prepare Tax Report - Canada

entry.title: Re: Part Period Balancing in PP/DS (SAP_PP_005 Heuristic)
entry.content:
Hi @Azk. Thanks for your feedback. As you mentioned this is very basic interpretetion. If you are more involved in cost-based modeling, you can also check out my PPO article: Production Planning Optimization (PPO) - Part I - SAP Community

I would love to have your comments on that as well.

entry.title: Quedada SAP - Let's talk about Joule - Mexico City 🇲🇽 #SAPCodeJamsLATAM
entry.content:
The local SAP Community in Mexico City has a chance to meet after our first of two local SAP CodeJams!

Quedada SAP in Mexico City will happen on August 7th, 2024 at 18:00 local time for a few hours, so join us and be a part of our Group!  You don't have to be an attendee of our SAP CodeJam events, just someone active or curious about the SAP Community and our focus on #developers

Let’s talk about what's new in the SAP world, about interesting SAP projects in the region, and what's new with ourselves.

Please let us know if you will participate in SAP Stammtisch by clicking "I will attend", we have a table reservation for 12 at Ulama, Antonio Dovali Jaime 70, Santa Fe, Zedec Sta Fé, Álvaro Obregón, 01218 Ciudad de México, CDMX, Mexico.  FYI, everyone is responsible for their own to buy a food/drink item if they wish, we are just happy for the chance to connect in person and have some relaxing conversation!

Click here to find more information about the SAP Developer Advocates second Latin America Roadshow

entry.title: Re: Improved Authorization Concept for Business Rules with 1H 2025 Release
entry.content:
Hi @MartinPosinger ,

I really appreciate your feedback.

I've improved the What's New Viewer topic so that it's hopefully much clearer how to configure the object definition of the "Rule" object.

The improved topic is going to be available on June 20th.

Kind regards,

Pierre

But I was informed that we do get certain number of certification attempts for free after taking the subscription. Is it not?

Best Regards
Arun

entry.title: Re: Cayman (KY) calculation procedure
entry.content:
Hi @0819 

You can copy the procedure and maintain.

You don't need to create zero tax but in case of transactions where you need a assign a tax code even its zero tax, you can use tax procedure and create zero tax codes. 

 

Regards

Vijaykumar

entry.title: Re: Im trying to distribute OD to my warehouse but its ge...

entry.title: Re: MDG Mass Processing Address Usage Error
entry.content: To solve the issue, please check SAP Note 3372801 "Upgrade or Conversion for Master Data Governance, Central Governance".

entry.title: Universal Parallel Accounting - UPA
entry.content:
Join us for a session to learn more about Universal Parallel Accounting (UPA)!
The event is organized by USF and is designed to help you understand UPA in depth, from key concepts to implementation strategies.

Agenda:

  • Introduction Universal Parallel Accounting
  • Parallel valuation from a group perspective and Parallel Valuation from a legal point of view
    • Consolidation
    • Intercompany matching and reconciliation
  • Move to UPA
  • Impact of UPA activation
  • The UPA road map for the different versions On premise/private cloud and Public cloud

Register here: https://usf.mynelis.com/extranet/public/events/492

Don't miss out—secure your spot today!

entry.title: Re: An Introduction to SAP Grants Management for Grantee
entry.content:
Hello Atif,

One question from my side is how to apply a cost charing formula whereas sponsors are mulitple.

As an example Grants are financed by several nations or adminstrations like for the EU member states participating into on sponsor program and every MS providing an amount depending on votes done at governmental level?

BE 3 Mio euros

DE 15 Mio euros

FR 12 Mio euros

etc...

entry.title: Re: App "API Business Hub Experience" is not accessible f...
entry.content: The "API Business Hub Enterprise" was renamed to "Developer Hub"

entry.title: Re: On-Premise AI Excellence: How SAP HANA EE and Skybuffer AI Deliver
entry.content: Replied as a private message.

entry.title: How students can get certified free and FAST in SAP (Less than 7 hours!)
entry.content:
Hi Everyone, 

I’d like to share information about a new type of certification available for SAP partners, employees, and students. This certification, known as the business certification, is now accessible free of charge through the SAP Learning Hub, student edition. The name of the certification is "Positioning SAP Business Suite," and it provides a comprehensive overview of the SAP Business Suite concept. 

This certification covers how consultants align SAP Business Suite features with customer infrastructure and communicate effectively to ensure a successful implementation. It's a fantastic introduction to SAP's offerings and an efficient way to earn an SAP certification. With just one or two days of study, you can enhance your CV with this new credential. 

 

Step 1: Register for your free subscription 

 To begin, the first step is to successfully verify and sign up for SAP Learning Hub, student edition. I have detailed the full process for registration here in this blog to guide you through it. 

 

Step 2: Find the content you need to study 

 Once your registration is complete and your subscription is activated, the SAP Business Suite learning journey and certification will become visible within the Student Zone upon logging in with your newly activated free subscription. (Scroll down a bit and you will see it in the first content carousel.) 

Alternately, you can access the two relevant courses here: 

1. Discovering SAP for Students 

2. Positioning SAP Business Suite for Students (This is the content gated for successful registrants to SAP Learning Hub, student edition. You can’t see it until go you through this process.) 

 

Step 3: Start studying and do the practical exercise 

You can get yourself familiar with SAP solutions through hands-on practice based on a real business case. In this video, you can see how it works and some tips on the first step of the practice. 

 

 Note: Most of the knowledge required to pass the exam can be acquired through the courses listed above. However, I recommend you better familiarize yourself with SAP Business Data Cloud before proceeding - this is the most difficult component of the exam for some students. Read more here: Positioning SAP Business Data Cloud. 

 

Step 4: Scheduling your exam  

After completing the practical exercise, your ability to schedule your exam will be unlocked.  

Follow the example demo here but, be sure to use the exam code for SAP Business Suite instead of what you see in the demo. 

 If at any point you need support with the process, please go to the SAP Certification support page. 

 Best of luck and congratulations in advance for earning your first official SAP credential. 

Regards, 

David

entry.title: Change learningCenterUrl parameter for SAP Enable Now
entry.content:
Hello,

we want the learning center button in the Sap Companion to redirect to a SAP Enable Now Book Page that we created.

I found this post from https://enablenowexpert.com/wiki/index.php/LearningCenterUrl but did not understand where to find the learningCenterUrl parameter and how to change it ?

Thanks in advance for your help

entry.title: Re: Impact convertion ECC to S/4HANA in SAP WM
entry.content:
@daniel_cunha1 

Yellow icon means that there is configuration of a component out of the scope. Red icon means that there is transactional data of a component out of the scope.

You have a yellow icon for wave management. It means your warehouses are customized for wave management, which is out of the stock room management scope. Most probably the configuration is an artefact of the warehouses setup when they had been copied from the SAP standard warehouse 001.

The STOCKROOM_COMPLIANCE_CHECK report check for wave management configuration with the is_customizings_wave_mgnt_used method:

  METHOD is_customizings_wave_mgnt_used.
    DATA: lv_lgnum      TYPE lgnum.

    SELECT lgnum FROM t340d INTO lv_lgnum UP TO 1 ROWS WHERE lgnum = iv_warehouse_number AND nukrf <> ''.ENDSELECT.
    IF sy-subrc = 0.
      rv_status = 'X'.
    ENDIF.
  ENDMETHOD.

I think you can leave the settings as they are without a risk of licensing issues. However if you want to be 100% sure that your setup is stock room management compliant go to OMLW configuration and remove group/wave number ranges from all warehouses:

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Error-Unable to fetch Offer Detail Template Id
entry.content:
Hi Team- Unable to fetch Offer Detail Template Id

entry.title: Re: Background Creation of Production versions
entry.content:
Good morning,

well, I actually hoped that if SAP makes PVs mandatory, they would also offer comprehensive tools to create, maintain and delete them (btw., haven't seen a mass deletion function yet, either). Our IT strategy currently goes towards trying to have less Z-implementations and more use of standard. 

For the migration program you mentioned I also found out that if you exclude routings in status 9 it will not propose production versions that refer to only a BOM (with a plan in status 4, which has a deletion flag and hence should not be considered). This program doesn't seem technically mature either.

Dana

Note: refer to the relevant sections in the Enhancement Spot ES_SAPLMIGO, starting with the flow logic of subscreen 360.

entry.title: Re: Behavior of Smart Multi Input paste value from excel
entry.content: Tried the above example and for me everything works fine. Might be fixed by now.

entry.title: Re: Downloading Your Business Configuration Adaptations in SAP Central Business Configuration
entry.content:
Hello JOB2,

Thanks for your question. For such cases, we recommend the Availability and dependencies of solution processes .xlsx file that gives you more information about scenarios and their availabilities and requirements. To get there, follow this path:

In SAP Process Navigator, navigate to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition > Accelerators > Getting Started  > Availability and dependencies of solution processes .xlsx file > Scope_BP_CLD_ENTPR sheet > For example, check columns G and J etc.

I've checked 1QM, which can't be directly selected in SAP Central Business Configuration because it requires an additional SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition license. You can create a support case under component FIN-FSCM-CR-CNT to request it so that it can be added to scope and then be activated. Please note that the BD9, BDN, BJE, 3D2, and 3MO scenarios are required to be in scope.

Best regards, 
Fabian

Thanks again!

Best,
mo_97

entry.title: Scheduling the secondary backup to be triggered on On-premises server
entry.content:
we want to schedule daily new full backup on on-premise server -->> they want to know how they can back their systems up to the on-premise storage from AWS.

 

"We have the setup of full backup and incremental backup scheduled from DB13 to hana database server filesystem daily, and it will then be moved to S3 bucket.

but now as per the audit requirement the full backup to be scheduled regularly from SAP/Hana to on-premise server and this backup should be able to have the restore successfully whenever needed. Need your help in understanding and scheduling the second backups to be trigged regularly without disturbing the present backup setup. 

Please help to provide the pre-requisites and steps to be followed to run the full backup on On-premises servers."

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Scheduling the secondary backup to be triggered on On...
entry.content:
To schedule and manage full backups from your AWS-hosted SAP HANA database to an on-premise server without disturbing your current backup setup, you can follow these steps:

Prerequisites:

  1. Network Connectivity: Ensure that your on-premise server has network connectivity to your AWS environment. This may involve setting up a VPN or Direct Connect.
  2. Storage Capacity: Ensure that your on-premise server has sufficient storage capacity to hold the full backups.
  3. Backup Software: Install and configure backup software that can handle the transfer of backups from AWS to your on-premise server. This could be a tool like rsync, SCP, or a more sophisticated backup solution.
  4. Permissions: Ensure that the necessary permissions are in place for the on-premise server to access the AWS environment and vice versa.

Steps to Schedule Full Backups:Step 1: Configure Network Connectivity

  • Set up a secure connection between your AWS environment and your on-premise server using VPN or AWS Direct Connect.
  • Ensure that the on-premise server can access the HANA database server and the S3 bucket.

Step 2: Install Backup Software

  • Install backup software on the on-premise server that can handle the transfer of files from AWS.
  • Configure the software to authenticate and connect to the AWS environment.

Step 3: Create Backup Scripts

  • Create a script on the HANA database server to perform the full backup. This script should be similar to your existing backup script but should target the on-premise server.
  • Example script to create a full backup:
    #!/bin/bash
BACKUP_DIR="/path/to/backup/directory"
BACKUP_NAME="hana_full_backup_$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S).tar.gz"
BACKUP_PATH="$BACKUP_DIR/$BACKUP_NAME"

# Perform the full backup
hdbsql -u <username> -p <password> -i <instance> "BACKUP DATA FOR FULL SYSTEM USING FILE ('$BACKUP_PATH')"

# Transfer the backup to the on-premise server
scp $BACKUP_PATH user@on-premise-server:/path/to/on-premise/backup/directory/

Step 4: Schedule the Backup

  • Use a scheduling tool like cron to schedule the backup script to run daily.
  • Example cron job to run the backup script at midnight every day:
    0 0 * * * /path/to/backup/script.sh

Step 5: Verify Backup and Restore

  • After the first backup is completed, verify that the backup file is successfully transferred to the on-premise server.
  • Perform a test restore from the on-premise backup to ensure that the backup is valid and can be restored successfully.

Additional Considerations:

  • Monitoring: Set up monitoring to ensure that backups are completed successfully and that any issues are promptly addressed.
  • Security: Ensure that the backup files are encrypted during transfer and at rest on the on-premise server.
  • Retention Policy: Define a retention policy for the backups on the on-premise server to manage storage usage.

By following these steps, you can schedule and manage full backups from your AWS-hosted SAP HANA database to an on-premise server without disturbing your current backup setup.

entry.title: Unable to configure US Benefits in SuccessFactors Sales demo instance.
entry.content:
Hi All

We have already EC Core setup done in the SuccessFactors Sales Demo instance. We are trying to run the upgrade for US Benefits Localizations in the upgrade center.

There are prerequisites for US Benefits Localization which are EC US localization and EC core setup. Since EC core setup was already done, I am not sure why it is showing as pending prerequisites on the upgrade page. I ran the EC Core again and below is the message for the issue.


We got the below error message:

-----------------------------------------------

[Best Practices Configurations Employee Central Core (XX) Begins, The value of Picklist migration is true. Will continue the upgrade with MDF Picklists, -------Preparing for Upgrade------ , Provisioning access granted to the user, ------Performing Upgrade------- , 'Employee Central V2' is activated in Provisioning. Now the upgrade can continue,
BPMsg: There are existing foundation objects: EVENT_REASON. Upgrade cannot continue, Upgrade Failed; All changes will rollback.
For further help with this error, please raise a ticket in the component LOD-SF-SBP.]

----------------------------------------

Please share your experiences and thoughts on the next steps to resolve.

entry.title: update button can not edit after uploading latest available ST-CONT file
entry.content:
Dear SAP

 

We can't use the update button on transaction Solman_setup / Application operations / System Monitoring / Step 2.7  In Solution Manager 7.2

we already upload the content zip manually


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: update button can not edit after uploading latest ava...
entry.content:
Keep in mind not all ST-CONT versions are applicable for all SAP Solution Manager Support Packages.

For example if you download STCONT20049_0-70000780.zip you can check file CSU_INFO.XML which shows the target versions to apply this content, in this case it is from Support Package 5 to Support Package 14 :

 

<CSU_DELY_CONTENT_METADATA>
<CONTENT_NAME>MAI</CONTENT_NAME>
<CONT_REG_VERSION>2</CONT_REG_VERSION>
<CONT_MAJ_VERSION>0</CONT_MAJ_VERSION>
<CONT_MIN_VERSION>0</CONT_MIN_VERSION>
<VALID_SP_VERSION>5</VALID_SP_VERSION>
.
.
.
<CSU_DELY_CONTENT_METADATA>
<CONTENT_NAME>MAI</CONTENT_NAME>
<CONT_REG_VERSION>2</CONT_REG_VERSION>
<CONT_MAJ_VERSION>0</CONT_MAJ_VERSION>
<CONT_MIN_VERSION>0</CONT_MIN_VERSION>
<VALID_SP_VERSION>14</VALID_SP_VERSION>
From STCONT20050_0-70000780.ZIP you can observe valid versions are from Support Package 12 until Support Package 14:
<CSU_DELY_CONTENT_METADATA>
<CONTENT_NAME>MAI_SYS_MON</CONTENT_NAME>
<CONT_REG_VERSION>2</CONT_REG_VERSION>
<CONT_MAJ_VERSION>0</CONT_MAJ_VERSION>
<CONT_MIN_VERSION>0</CONT_MIN_VERSION>
<VALID_SP_VERSION>12</VALID_SP_VERSION>
.
.
<CSU_DELY_CONTENT_METADATA>
<CONTENT_NAME>MAI</CONTENT_NAME>
<CONT_REG_VERSION>2</CONT_REG_VERSION>
<CONT_MAJ_VERSION>0</CONT_MAJ_VERSION>
<CONT_MIN_VERSION>0</CONT_MIN_VERSION>
<VALID_SP_VERSION>14</VALID_SP_VERSION>
So make sure to review CSU_INFO.XML file and upload only valid content for current version, however we encourage you to fully upgrade the SAP Solution Manager system to latest version to get latest available content updates.

entry.title: Re: Downloading Your Business Configuration Adaptations in SAP Central Business Configuration
entry.content:
Thanks Fabian for you update!

Best regards

Jorge

entry.title: Incorrect Cost on Partial Finished Goods Receipt in Production Order (Full Raw Materials Issued)
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I’m testing a production scenario in SAP and need help understanding why the cost behavior during a partial goods receipt seems incorrect.

Here’s the setup:

  • I created a production order to produce 100 units of finished goods.

  • The BOM requires 100 units of raw material, which I issued all at once to the production order. The total raw material cost is $1,000.

  • Then, I posted a partial goods receipt to inventory for 50 finished goods.

However, when I check the material document, I see that the inventory value for the 50 units received is $1,000, which is the full cost of all the raw materials.
But this doesn’t seem right — I only received half of the finished goods, so I expected the inventory to be valued at $500, based on 50 raw materials.

 

Why is SAP posting the full $1,000 cost to inventory even though only 50 units were received?
How can I make the system post only the proportional cost ($500) when receiving the partial quantity?

Thanks in advance for any guidance!

entry.title: ABAP in the Cloud: Debugging authorization
entry.content:
Dear all, 

i am using ABAP Cloud enviroment where i have developed a RAP Service. For some testing reasons i am debugging the service with EXTERNAL user in ADT. That works just fine. The issue what i have is that i am not authorized to change the variables under the debugger. 

My questions are: 

- How can i run some kind of authorization trace to check what is missing?

- Do somebody knows already a role which allows that?

I have already the proper DEVELOPER role assigned which was created by SAP. 

Anyone faced this issue? 

Thanks, 

Henryk

entry.title: Re: How to use batch/background job in procurement.
entry.content:
Could you please specify your requirements, as they are not clear?

  • Automatic creation of purchase orders (POs) from purchase requests (PRs).
  • MRP/DRP implementation?

For S/4HANA Cloud Public

  • check the scope item Procurement of Direct Materials (‏J45‏).
  • Specific request, check APIs in Sourcing and Procurement

entry.title: PO Fully Invoiced but Commitment Still Showing in S_ALR_87013558 Report (WBS)
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

I'm facing a commitment inconsistency issue in SAP PS and need help identifying the root cause.

Scenario:

  • WBS Element: 

  • Purchase Order: Fully invoiced, all GR/IR posted.

  • However, in the report S_ALR_87013558 – Actual/Commitments/Total, there is still a balance showing under commitment.

What I've already checked:

  1. In ME23N, the PO is fully invoiced, and PO history shows GR and IR fully matched.

  2. The Final Invoice checkbox was not ticked initially, but has been updated now.

  3. Ran CJCF (Commitment Carry Forward) – no issues found in the log.

  4. Ran CJEN in both test and update mode to reconstruct commitment data.

  5. Still, the commitment amount continues to appear in the report, although CJI5 shows no open commitment line items.

What I need help with:

  • Why would the commitment still show in S_ALR_87013558 even after CJEN and CJCF?

  • Is there any other reconciliation or consistency check I should run (e.g., RKANBU01 or RKACOR04)?

  • Could it be due to document archiving or CO inconsistency?

Additional Notes:

  • SAP ECC 

  • Any suggestions or SAP Notes related to this issue would be highly appreciated.

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Re: SuccesFactors Compensation - Compensation Inbox Info...
entry.content: Thank you @xavierlegarrec! Can you give me more info about this ad hoc report? Should I create a story, table etc.? Have you got any suggestion? Thank you in advance, Olga

entry.title: Executing BW Queries in background mode
entry.content:
Hi Community, 

We are using SAP S/4 HANA On premise with Fiori. 

I am looking for ways to improve query performance. Some queries which are created with the fiori app: "custom analytical queries" are taking too long to load, resulting in browser errors. Therefore, I am also looking for ways to execute these BW queries in the background. Either via SAP GUI or SAP Fiori. Can anyone help me? 

Via other community posts I see the transaction RSBATCH coming up several times but the transaction is not very user friendly. Is this the right way forward?  


Many thanks in advance.

entry.title: Template Allocation
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

We have to allocate overheads through Template Allocation with environment 009 (process order) with the base as Order Total Quantity. We have completed the below processes:

1. Created Overhead Key

2. Mapped Overhead Key to the Overhead Group

3. Maintained Overhead Group in Material Masters

4. Created Costing Sheet

5. Created the Template

6. Assigned Template to Costing Sheet, Overhead Group, Plant combination

But while running CK11N for the material, Template is not getting picked.

Kindly advise whether we are missing any configurations.

Thanks in advance!

Regards

Merin

entry.title: Re: Production Planning Optimization (PPO) - Part I
entry.content: Thank you @KN5, your feedback encourages me to continue 😊

entry.title: Discrepancy in Amounts Between SAP FM FBS and BCS Reports Post-Migration
entry.content:
Hi SAP Experts,

I have encountered an issue comparing amounts in SAP FM between FBS and BCS after executing the FM migration from FBS to BCS.

In FBS, I use the report FMEQ, while in BCS, I use GRR3. The difference lies in the Assigned column:

- In the FBS report (FMEQ), the key figure for the annual budget (Assigned) is BUF04.

- In the BCS report (GRR3), there is only one key figure available: HLST.

Regarding characteristics:

- The FBS report contains only Value Type 46 – Payment Budget: Releases.

- In BCS, I created multiple value types including 65 Funds Commitment, 66 Transfer Postings, 95 Secondary Cost Postings (CO), 50 Purchase Requisitions, 51 Purchase Orders, 60 Parked Documents, 81 Funds Reservation, 54 Invoices, 57 Payments.

And ledgers such as Funds Management Payment Budget, Funds Management Commitment Budget, Funds Management Controlling.

 

I also checked other standard FBS reports provided by SAP, such as FMRP_RFFMAV01X – Annual Budget. There, I observed discrepancies between Hard Assigned Funds and Hard Commitment/Actual values. The Hard Commitment/Actual amount matches the Assigned column in the BCS report, while the Hard Assigned Funds matches the Assigned amount in the FBS report. This amount is puzzling because when tracing document numbers related to the budget, I can't identify the source generating the total amount shown in Hard Assigned Funds. All document amounts align with the Hard Commitment/Actual value.

Additionally, I reviewed another FBS standard report, FMRP_3FMB4006 – Overview of Payment Budget, which shows a budget type KBFC – Assigned Funds. This budget type does not appear in BCS.

My questions are:

1. Does the key figure of the report column difference is the root cause? If yes, how do I define this in Report Painter? Currently, I can only select element type "key figure with characteristics" but not "predefined key figure."

2. What is the equivalent value type in BCS for Value Type 46 (Payment Budget: Releases)? I have tested adding all available value types in BCS, but the amounts don't match those in the FBS report.

3. What exactly are Hard Assigned Funds? Where does this amount originate from? How can I display it in my BCS report?

4. Is the budget type KBFC a configuration? If yes, where is it maintained? If not configuration, how can I maintain this budget type in BCS?

I appreciate any guidance or suggestions to resolve this discrepancy.

Thank you in advance for your assistance.
Regards,

Dennis

entry.title: LMS - AG5 API
entry.content:
We have a requirement for building API from AG5 system to Successfactors LMS, where AG5 will pull over the below details using API: course id course title (all language) user id course completion status. Is there any KBA or pointers available for this? 

On another note, can we send the csv file for reports from Successfactors LMS to their SFTP. If yes, please provide the pointers. 

Thanks,

Radhika

entry.title: Re: Im trying to distribute OD to my warehouse but its ge...
entry.content: Sorry To be honest with you its hard for me to say for me that which scenario the header's error will pop up. secondly if i am not sure about error messages then I take help of technical team help in dubugging and checking the root cause of the error.

entry.title: Re: Python code error in dataflow
entry.content:
I believe there's a typo in your code, the isin function should be followed by parentheses () instead of brackets []
Like: df = data[data['gender'].isin(specific_name)]

Regards,

entry.title: Why Sap lock doesn't release automatically?
entry.content:
Hi, experts.
Why sap lock doesn't release automaticalluy after program executing?
It's released agter if i press on of the 'Back' bution on the toolbar on write screen.
Why so? Is it should released after program execution?

REPORT  zva_ex50.
data : wa_asset type anla.

select single * from anla
  into wa_asset
  where bukrs = 1000
  and   anln1 = '000000040000'.


CALL FUNCTION 'ENQUEUE_EANLA'
 EXPORTING
   MODE_ANLA            = 'E'
   MANDT                = SY-MANDT
   BUKRS                = wa_asset-bukrs
   ANLN1                = wa_asset-anln1
   ANLN2                = wa_asset-anln2
   X_BUKRS              = 'X'
   X_ANLN1              = 'X'
   X_ANLN2              = 'X'
   _SCOPE               = '3'
   _WAIT                = ' '
   _COLLECT             = ' '
 EXCEPTIONS
   FOREIGN_LOCK         = 1
   SYSTEM_FAILURE       = 2
   OTHERS               = 3
          .
IF sy-subrc <> 0.
* MESSAGE ID SY-MSGID TYPE SY-MSGTY NUMBER SY-MSGNO
*         WITH SY-MSGV1 SY-MSGV2 SY-MSGV3 SY-MSGV4.
else.
  perform assert_change.
  write '123'.
ENDIF.
form assert_change.
data : lt_ret type bapiret2,
       lgd    type bapi1022_feglg001,
       lgdx   type bapi1022_feglg001x.
lgd-descript = 'Test 2'.
lgdx-descript = 'X'.

CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_FIXEDASSET_CHANGE'
  EXPORTING
    companycode                = wa_asset-bukrs
    asset                      = wa_asset-anln1
    subnumber                  = wa_asset-anln2
   GENERALDATA                = lgd
   GENERALDATAX               = lgdx
 IMPORTING
   RETURN                     = lt_ret.

commit work and WAIT.
endform.

entry.title: SAP DM > SMQ2: Status type Staging Production (DMS) of item 20 not allowed for MES-Driven staging
entry.content:
**SMQ2: PMR not created for single order staging with DM / MES Connection (Status Profile DMS)**

Status type Staging Production (DMS) of item 20 not allowed for MES-Driven staging

Message No. /SCWM/PWR803

 

and others

***************************************

Hello,

for an improtant demo we need to show a staging 2.0 process with MES/ SAP DM and we are getting errors in smq2 which prebvent the ewm system to get an pmr created with single order staging (with requirements) in psastage.

I am checking this here

can anybody help here?

entry.title: Re: 🧰👷‍♂️ The SAP S/4HANA RISE & SAP BTP - Toolbox 👷‍♂️🧰
entry.content:
Changing Profile Parameters and Locking Profiles on S/4 on RISE / ECS and SAP Star SAP*

3460793 - [ECS] Cannot modify Default Profile in transaction (RZ10) in Business Clients - SAP for Me

3286240 - Client administrative actions restrictions (main note) - SAP for Me

deployment-strategies-for-the-kernel-of-the-sap-application-server-abap.pdf

3293607 - Client administrative actions restrictions in transaction RZ10 and SMICM - SAP for Me

3304209 - Client administrative actions restrictions for dynamic parameter changes - SAP for Me

3308883 - Client administrative actions restrictions for Server Status Change - SAP for Me

3441097 - Client administrative actions restrictions for Server Status Change - Shutdown control - SAP for Me

3517086 - Non-Security Parameters for ABAP systems in SAP Enterprise Cloud Services (ECS) - SAP for Me

3250501 - Information on mandatory Security Parameters & Hardening requirements for ABAP systems in SAP Enterprise Cloud Services (ECS) - SAP for Me

3089798 - ECS Brownfield setup pro-active checks - SAP for Me

3344326 - FAQ : Private Cloud Landscape (ECS Landscape) - SAP for Me

3351928 - FAQ : ECS ABAP system user management - SAP for Me Including SAP* SAP Star

3303172 - Activating a Super-User SAP* - SAP for Me

2333305 - Tutorial - "Restore emergency user SAP*" [VIDEO] - SAP for Me

Are there anything I need to investigate before I delete the users?

Thanks

Dorthe

entry.title: Re: How SAP Digital Manufacturing Simplifies Tool Management
entry.content:
Hello Manoel,

Thanks for the post.

It looks interesting; however, the link mentioned above indicates that the PRT must be assigned to the routing on the ERP side. If this is a prerequisite, it suggests that the new feature may still not be available for PP-PI.

While PRTs can be assigned in the recipe, they currently have no effect, as stated in OSS Note 350614.

Does this update address the needs of PP-PI users?

Thanks,

Emmanuel.

entry.title: Re: Drive Process-centric SAP S/4HANA Transformation with SAP Signavio
entry.content: Great post! Thank you!

entry.title: Re: No BAPI for Purchase Order Rejection? Here is the solution, like FIORI does!
entry.content:
Hi, 

I got below finding to reject a PO without using ME29N.

1. Use BAPI_PO_RESET_RELEASE ( This is only useful to reject PO after release )

2. Use EKKO-PROCSTAT (Release Status) logic where PROCSTAT=08 (Reject)

Hit like if it helped.

Thanks 

Faisal Mansoori

@jose_sequeira

entry.title: Re: BAPI/FM to Reject/Approve PO
entry.content:
Hi, 

I got below finding to reject a PO without using ME29N.

1. Use BAPI_PO_RESET_RELEASE ( This is only useful to reject PO after release )

2. Use EKKO-PROCSTAT (Release Status) logic where PROCSTAT=08 (Reject)

Hit like if it helped.

Thanks 

Faisal Mansoori

entry.title: Re: A look into the Migration Assessment Modernization Recommendation - Clean Core
entry.content: Great article, Denise! Thanks for sharing such valuable information.

entry.title: How to Perform Partial payment Scenario in Automatic Payment Program in SAP S4HANA public cloud
entry.content:
Hi All,

Could you Please provide guidance on how we can efficiently handle below scenarios within the Automatic Payment Program in SAP S4HANA Public Cloud?

  1. Partial payments against supplier invoices.
  2. Advance payments tied to specific purchase orders.
  3. Clearing specific partial amounts against individual supplier invoices.
  4. Holding a portion of invoice payments for a certain period before later clearing them against the same invoice.

#Automatic Payment Program # SAP S4HANA Public Cloud

entry.title: Re: SuccessFactors - Variable Pay Basis Calculation
entry.content: Hi xavierlegarrec,

entry.title: Re: SAP DRC can be connected to Multiple backend S/4 syst...
entry.content:
Yes, SAP Document and Reporting Compliance (DRC) can be connected to multiple backend SAP S/4HANA systems within the same landscape. 
How to connect SAP DRC to multiple S/4HANA systems:
  1. Multiple Participant IDs: You can configure multiple participant IDs in SAP DRC, where each participant ID is linked to a specific backend S/4HANA system.
  2. GLN as an Additional Participant ID: You can utilize Global Location Numbers (GLN) as additional participant IDs to identify each backend S/4HANA system uniquely. This allows for a flexible approach where you can generate and assign GLN numbers to your company as needed, enabling connections to multiple backend systems. 
Key considerations:
  • Licensing: Ensure that your SAP DRC license covers the required number of connections and entities for your multiple S/4HANA systems.
  • Configuration: Proper configuration of SAP DRC is crucial for seamless integration and data exchange with multiple backend systems.
  • Complexity: Managing multiple connections can increase the complexity of your system landscape. Consider using a well-defined naming convention and proper documentation for easier management. 
Benefits of connecting SAP DRC to multiple S/4HANA systems:
  • Centralized Compliance Management: Provides a single point of access for managing compliance requirements across your entire S/4HANA landscape.
  • Enhanced Efficiency: Reduces the need for separate compliance processes for each system, resulting in improved operational efficiency.
  • Improved Accuracy: Ensures consistent and accurate data for compliance reporting across all connected systems.

By leveraging these approaches, you can effectively connect SAP DRC to multiple backend S/4HANA systems in the same landscape and reap the benefits of centralized compliance management.

entry.title: Re: SuccessFactors - Variable Pay Basis Calculation
entry.content: Hi xavierlegarrec, Thank you so much for your prompt answer. I really really appreciate it. just quick question, does it also work for Scenario 2 if somebody got promoted during the year, the bonus will be prorated based on Merit Review as of 1st April, and prorated also with Promotion salary? It will be like two assignments; 1st Assignment (from January 1st to July 31st) with Merit review salary, and 2nd assignment (from 1st Aug to 31st Dec) with Promotion salary.

entry.title: Re: Clarification Needed: SAP Managed vs. SAP Hosted Foun...
entry.content:
Please have a look at the SAP note https://me.sap.com/notes/3437766

I am quoting from that note: 

Access Type

Models available on Generative AI Hub are accessed via remote providers, such as AWS Bedrock, or hosted by SAP AI Core.

SAP Hosted (sap-hosted): Model(s) hosted on SAP’s own infrastructure, managed by SAP AI Core.

SAP Managed (sap-managed): Model(s) hosted on SAP’s tenant isolated Hyperscaler infrastructure and managed by SAP AI Core.

Remote (provider e.g. AWS Model(s) hosted and managed by Providers, accessed via SAP AI Core.

entry.title: Re: Can'r create a new cycle using the newly created exte...
entry.content: Thank you for your reply.

entry.title: ‌🇧🇷‌ Virtual Session: Reforma Tributária - PwC & SAP - Parte III
entry.content:
Join us for the third webinar dedicated to the Tax Reform in Brazil!
This online session will be conducted by PwC and SAP and will cover key aspects of the Tax Reform, including:

  • New SAP Technical Notes

  • Event Status

  • Split Payment

Speakers:

Rafael Almeida - Senior Manager, PwC Brazil

Rodrigo Cabral - Senior Manager & Solutions Architect, PwC Brazil
Cláudia Wada - NF-e SAP Product Manager, SAP Brazil
Andréa Miele - SAP Product Manager, SAP Brazil
Cintia Ahymoto - SAP Product Manager, SAP Brazil
Eduardo Kyono - SAP Product Manager, SAP Brazil

This webinar will de held in Portuguese.

Don’t miss this opportunity to stay informed and prepared!

Registration link

If you want to get more information about the Tax Reform provided by SAP experts, join the Brazil - Tax Reform Localization Work Zone group.

entry.title: Re: how to add custom fields from MARA(using CFL) in GUI transactions in T-Code MM01/02/03
entry.content:
Hi @Yogananda,

Is there any specific configuration to be added, so the field added via Custom fields app to Business Context PRODUCT is visible in apps with SAP Gui Context RFM_PRODUCT_GUI ? Is it needed to extend/create manually screen and function group SAPLMGD1 (as it was reuqired in ECC)?

thx for suggestions,

entry.title: Unit test is diffrence between Cloud and on-premise
entry.content:
Dear experts, when I was testing Unit test in the local version of the system, I found that after using the commit entities statement, the data was persistently stored in the DB, and the ROLLBACK ENTITIES statement did not take effect. May I ask if this is a BUG? The following is my test code. The problem occurs in the 'check_input_is_validate' method:

"!@testing BDEF:ZI_UT_ROOT
CLASS ltc_ZI_UT_ROOT
DEFINITION FINAL FOR TESTING
DURATION SHORT
RISK LEVEL HARMLESS.

  PRIVATE SECTION.
    CLASS-DATA:cds_test_environment TYPE REF TO if_cds_test_environment,
               sql_test_environment TYPE REF TO if_osql_test_environment.

    CLASS-METHODS:
      "! Instantiate class under test and setup test double frameworks
      class_setup,

      "! Destroy test environments and test doubles
      class_teardown.

    METHODS:
      "! Reset test doubles
      setup,

      "! Reset transactional buffer
      teardown,

      "! Check input is validate
      check_input_is_validate FOR TESTING,

      "! Check input is invalid
      check_input_is_invalid FOR TESTING,

      "! Check returned features for instance with overall status 'Open'
      check_setstatustoopen      FOR TESTING,

      "! Check returned features for instance with overall status 'Rejected'
      get_features_rejected      FOR TESTING,

      "! Check action result is right
      check_action_result FOR TESTING.

ENDCLASS.


CLASS ltc_ZI_UT_ROOT IMPLEMENTATION.

  METHOD class_setup.
    cds_test_environment = cl_cds_test_environment=>create( i_for_entity = 'ZI_UT_ROOT' ).
    sql_test_environment = cl_osql_test_environment=>create( i_dependency_list = VALUE #( ( 'I_SUPPLIER_VH' ) ) ).
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD class_teardown.
    cds_test_environment->destroy( ).
    sql_test_environment->destroy( ).
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD check_setstatustoopen.
    MODIFY ENTITIES OF zi_ut_root
        ENTITY zi_ut_root
        CREATE FIELDS ( Status )
        WITH VALUE #( ( %cid   = 'Root'
                        Status = ''  ) )
        MAPPED DATA(mapped)
        FAILED DATA(faild)
        REPORTED DATA(reported).

    READ ENTITIES OF zi_ut_root
        ENTITY zi_ut_root
        ALL FIELDS WITH
        CORRESPONDING #( mapped-zi_ut_root )
        RESULT DATA(root_results).

    cl_abap_unit_assert=>assert_equals( msg = 'Status should be O!' exp = 'O' act = root_results[ 1 ]-Status ).
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD get_features_rejected.
    MODIFY ENTITIES OF zi_ut_root
        ENTITY zi_ut_root
        CREATE FIELDS ( Status )
        WITH VALUE #( ( %cid   = 'Root'
                        Status = 'R'  ) )
        MAPPED DATA(mapped)
        FAILED DATA(faild)
        REPORTED DATA(reported).

    MODIFY ENTITIES OF zi_ut_root
        ENTITY zi_ut_root
            EXECUTE rejectOrder
            FROM VALUE #( ( %tky = mapped-zi_ut_root[ 1 ]-%tky  ) )
        FAILED DATA(failed_action)
        REPORTED DATA(reported_action).

    cl_abap_unit_assert=>assert_equals( act = lines( failed_action-zi_ut_root ) exp = 1 msg = 'Should has error' ).
    cl_abap_unit_assert=>assert_not_initial( act = failed_action-zi_ut_root[ 1 ]-Orderid ).
    cl_abap_unit_assert=>assert_equals( exp = if_abap_behv=>mk-on
                                        act = failed_action-zi_ut_root[ 1 ]-%action-rejectOrder
                                        msg = 'rejectOrder action should be disabled').
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD setup.
    cds_test_environment->clear_doubles( ).
    sql_test_environment->clear_doubles( ).
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD check_action_result.
    MODIFY ENTITIES OF zi_ut_root
        ENTITY zi_ut_root
        CREATE FIELDS ( TotalPrice )
        WITH VALUE #( ( %cid   = 'Root'
                        TotalPrice = '100'  ) )
        ENTITY zi_ut_root
            EXECUTE deductDiscount
            FROM VALUE #( ( %cid_ref = 'Root'
                            %param-discount_percent = '10' ) )
        ENTITY zi_ut_root
            EXECUTE acceptOrder
            FROM VALUE #( ( %cid_ref = 'Root' ) )
        MAPPED DATA(mapped)
        FAILED DATA(faild)
        REPORTED DATA(reported).

    READ ENTITIES OF zi_ut_root
       ENTITY zi_ut_root
       ALL FIELDS WITH
       CORRESPONDING #( mapped-zi_ut_root )
       RESULT DATA(root_results).

    cl_abap_unit_assert=>assert_equals( act = lines( root_results ) exp = 1 msg = 'Should has result' ).
    cl_abap_unit_assert=>assert_equals( act = root_results[ 1 ]-TotalPrice exp = '90' msg = 'Total price should be 90' ).
    cl_abap_unit_assert=>assert_equals( act = root_results[ 1 ]-Status exp = 'A' msg = 'Status should be A' ).

  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD teardown.
    ROLLBACK ENTITIES.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD check_input_is_validate.
    DATA vendor_mock_data TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF i_supplier_vh.

    vendor_mock_data = VALUE #( ( Supplier = 'test' ) ).
    sql_test_environment->insert_test_data( vendor_mock_data ).

    MODIFY ENTITIES OF zi_ut_root
        ENTITY zi_ut_root
        CREATE FIELDS ( Status vendor BeginDate EndDate )
        WITH VALUE #( ( %cid   = 'Root'
                        Status = ''
                        vendor = 'test'
                        BeginDate = cl_abap_context_info=>get_system_date( ) - 10
                        EndDate = cl_abap_context_info=>get_system_date( ) + 10 ) )
    MAPPED DATA(mapped)
    FAILED DATA(faild)
    REPORTED DATA(reported).

*    COMMIT ENTITIES IN SIMULATION MODE RESPONSES
    COMMIT ENTITIES RESPONSES
        FAILED DATA(commit_failed)
        REPORTED DATA(commit_reported).

    cl_abap_unit_assert=>assert_initial( act = commit_failed msg = `Should't has error` ).
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD check_input_is_invalid.
    DATA vendor_mock_data TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF i_supplier_vh.

    vendor_mock_data = VALUE #( ( Supplier = 'test' ) ).
    sql_test_environment->insert_test_data( vendor_mock_data ).

    MODIFY ENTITIES OF zi_ut_root
        ENTITY zi_ut_root
        CREATE FIELDS ( Status vendor BeginDate EndDate )
        WITH VALUE #( ( %cid   = 'Root'
                        Status = 'X'
                        vendor = 'test_1'
                        BeginDate = cl_abap_context_info=>get_system_date( ) + 10
                        EndDate = cl_abap_context_info=>get_system_date( ) - 10 ) )
    MAPPED DATA(mapped)
    FAILED DATA(faild)
    REPORTED DATA(reported).

    COMMIT ENTITIES IN SIMULATION MODE RESPONSE OF zi_ut_root
        FAILED DATA(commit_failed)
        REPORTED DATA(commit_reported).

    cl_abap_unit_assert=>assert_not_initial( act = commit_reported-zi_ut_root[ %element-status = if_abap_behv=>mk-on ]
                                             msg = 'Status should be marked as error' ).
    cl_abap_unit_assert=>assert_not_initial( act = commit_reported-zi_ut_root[ %element-vendor = if_abap_behv=>mk-on ]
                                             msg = 'Vendor should be marked as error' ).
    cl_abap_unit_assert=>assert_not_initial( act = commit_reported-zi_ut_root[ %element-BeginDate = if_abap_behv=>mk-on ]
                                             msg = 'BeginDate should be marked as error' ).
    cl_abap_unit_assert=>assert_not_initial( act = commit_reported-zi_ut_root[ %element-EndDate = if_abap_behv=>mk-on ]
                                             msg = 'EndDate should be marked as error' ).
  ENDMETHOD.

ENDCLASS.

 BDEF Code：

managed implementation in class zbp_i_ut_root unique;
strict ( 2 );
with draft;

define behavior for zi_ut_root //alias <alias_name>
persistent table zrap_ut_root
draft table zrap_ut_root_df
lock master total etag LastChangedAt
authorization master ( instance )
early numbering
//etag master <field_name>
{
  create;
  update ( features : instance );
  delete ( features : instance );

  draft action ( features : instance ) Edit;
  draft action Activate optimized;
  draft action Discard;
  draft action Resume;
  draft determine action Prepare
  {
    validation checkInput;
  }

  determination setStatusToOpen on modify { create; update; }

  validation checkInput on save { create; update; field Status, Vendor, BeginDate, EndDate; }

  action ( features : instance ) deductDiscount parameter zp_ut_root_discount result [1] $self;
  action ( features : instance ) acceptOrder;
  action ( features : instance ) rejectOrder;

  side effects
  {
    action acceptOrder affects $self;
    action rejectOrder affects $self;
  }

  field ( readonly )
  Orderid,
  CreatedBy,
  CreatedAt,
  LocalLastChangedBy,
  LocalLastChangedAt,
  LastChangedAt;

  field ( mandatory )
  Status,
  Vendor,
  BeginDate,
  EndDate;

  association _item { create; with draft; }

  mapping for zrap_ut_root control zrap_ut_root_ct
    {
      Orderid            = orderid;
      Status             = status;
      Vendor             = vendor;
      BeginDate          = begin_date;
      EndDate            = end_date;
      TotalPrice         = total_price;
      CurrencyCode       = currency_code;
      CreatedBy          = created_by;
      CreatedAt          = created_at;
      LocalLastChangedBy = local_last_changed_by;
      LocalLastChangedAt = local_last_changed_at;
      LastChangedAt      = last_changed_at;
    }
}

define behavior for zi_ut_item //alias <alias_name>
persistent table zrap_ut_item
draft table zrap_ut_item_df
lock dependent by _root
authorization dependent by _root
early numbering
//etag master <field_name>
{
  update;
  delete;
  field ( readonly )
  Orderid,
  Itemid,
  CreatedBy,
  CreatedAt,
  LocalLastChangedBy,
  LocalLastChangedAt,
  LastChangedAt;

  association _root { with draft; }
  association _subitm { create; with draft; }

  mapping for zrap_ut_item control zrap_ut_item_ct
    {
      Orderid            = orderid;
      Itemid             = itemid;
      Component          = component;
      Quantity           = quantity;
      Unit               = unit;
      CreatedBy          = created_by;
      CreatedAt          = created_at;
      LocalLastChangedBy = local_last_changed_by;
      LocalLastChangedAt = local_last_changed_at;
      LastChangedAt      = last_changed_at;
    }
}

define behavior for zi_ut_subitm //alias <alias_name>
persistent table zrap_ut_subitm
draft table zrap_ut_sub_df
lock dependent by _root
authorization dependent by _root
early numbering
//etag master <field_name>
{
  update;
  delete;
  field ( readonly )
  Orderid,
  Itemid,
  Subitemid,
  CreatedBy,
  CreatedAt,
  LocalLastChangedBy,
  LocalLastChangedAt,
  LastChangedAt;

  association _root { with draft; }
  association _item { with draft; }

  mapping for zrap_ut_subitm control zrap_ut_subitm_ct
    {
      Orderid            = orderid;
      Itemid             = itemid;
      Subitemid          = subitemid;
      ScheduleDate       = schedule_date;
      Quantity           = quantity;
      Unit               = unit;
      Customer           = customer;
      CreatedBy          = created_by;
      CreatedAt          = created_at;
      LocalLastChangedBy = local_last_changed_by;
      LocalLastChangedAt = local_last_changed_at;
      LastChangedAt      = last_changed_at;
    }
}

Root entity code:

@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'Interface for Unit test --Root view'
@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true
define root view entity zi_ut_root
  as select from zrap_ut_root
  composition [0..*] of zi_ut_item as _item
  association to I_Supplier_VH     as _supp on $projection.Vendor = _supp.Supplier
{
      @EndUserText.label: 'ID'
  key orderid               as Orderid,
      status                as Status,
      vendor                as Vendor,
      _supp.SupplierName    as VendorName,
      begin_date            as BeginDate,
      end_date              as EndDate,
      @Semantics.amount.currencyCode: 'CurrencyCode'
      total_price           as TotalPrice,
      currency_code         as CurrencyCode,
      @Semantics.user.createdBy: true
      created_by            as CreatedBy,
      @Semantics.systemDateTime.createdAt: true
      created_at            as CreatedAt,
      @Semantics.user.lastChangedBy: true
      local_last_changed_by as LocalLastChangedBy,
      @Semantics.systemDateTime.localInstanceLastChangedAt: true
      local_last_changed_at as LocalLastChangedAt,
      @Semantics.systemDateTime.lastChangedAt: true
      last_changed_at       as LastChangedAt,
      //    _association_name // Make association public

      _item,
      _supp
}

When I perform the unit test, this test data will be persistently stored IN the DB. I think this is incorrect unless I use COMMIT ENTITIES IN SIMULATION MODE RESPONSES instead of COMMIT ENTITIES RESPONSES，Can anyone help me solve this doubt? Thank you very much.

entry.title: Re: SAP DRC can be connected to Multiple backend S/4 syst...
entry.content: Thanks for the information, Do we any guide to refer with respect to integration or SAP Notes?

entry.title: Re: How Analytics is Making Basketball a More Beautiful Game
entry.content: Nice insight Elliot

entry.title: Split Budget for Merit & Adjustment Global Template
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We are implementing Compensation for 33 countries. In some countries they required Budget based on (Merit + Adjustment) and in some countries ( Merit & Adjustment budget needs to split for e.g. Total Budget is 3% and out of that Merit Budget is 2% and adjustment is 1%).

Do I need to create two separate template for the same or within one template it can be accommodated.

Please provide your inputs on the same.

@xavierlegarrec 

Regards,

Satish

entry.title: Re: Concat two dimension properties and pass it to booked...
entry.content:
Hi @William_Yu

My idea was to filter a table on those two dimensions = "#". Then make an "on button click" script in an optimized story to do as explained in original post. As I can, string manipulations is available from there? However not sure if I can change the member combinations?

entry.title: Re: Python code error in dataflow
entry.content:
Hi XaviPolo

Thanks for reply.

I have modified the code as you mentioend,however I'm still getting a syntax error as below.

def transform(data)
specific_name = ['Male']
df = data[data['gender'].isin(specific_name)]
return df

Kind regards

Hari

entry.title: BTP Trial IAS Provisioning Issue
entry.content: “My BTP trial global account ID is 58dc44e9trial in us10. Identity Authentication token URL exists, but the admin UI and SAML metadata both return errors (Uh oh, 404). I'm unable to establish trust to subscribe to SAP Build Work Zone. Please advise.”

entry.title: CPQ 2.0 - OnItemCopied event (global script)
entry.content:
In “Before add to quote” event of my configurable product I create a unique id ("PA_UniqueId) an put it in an attribute (if attribute is empty). In a global script on the event "BeforeItemAdded" , I read that attribute and add it to a Quote Item Custom field (UCF_UniqueId)

Now when I copy that Item on the Cart, this attribute and user custom field are copied along. What I want to do is to fill them with a new unique id (because otherwise it is not unique anymore)

This I try to do in a global script with event "OnItemCopied". There I get the product from the "context.CopiedItems" and fill the attribute with a new unique id. I do the same for the user custom field of the copiedItem. The Orginal Items I leave as it is.

My question now is, how can this be saved ? Because the values are not stored (although if I debug them at the end of the script, the new values are visible in the attribute / user custom field)

Next info was found in the HELP

User copies item in quote
          Execute item action : Copy
                Performing calculations on copied item
                Build number of items for product types
                Copying product configuration
                Copying item attachments
          Saving copied items
  After copying products in quote event trigered
 context:  CopiedItems : IEnumerable<IQuoteItem>
                 OriginalItems : IEnumerable<IQuoteItem>
                 Quote : Instance of type: Quote2
                Get item column values
  Before quote rendering event trigered
context -> Quote : Instance of type: Quote2

As far as I can see the product / quote item is not saved anymore. So what is the use of the "After copying products in quote event" to be triggered ? Some more info on how this works, and what the use is would be nice. 

Who can tell me what I'm doing wrong  and how I can solve my problem ?

entry.title: Re: CJ42 S4 HANA Fiori Apps
entry.content: Thanks. This helps a lot.

entry.title: Re: Send Different Activation Emails to Newly Created Users Based on Their Account Attributes
entry.content:
Hi everyone

Do you know if we can include the user id from lms (login name in ias) into the ias mail text?

KR
Olga

entry.title: Re: SBM closing bill process is taking more than expected
entry.content:
Hello Alberto,

the closing of the bill is an asynchronous process. Usually you'll receive a 200 for single bills closing or a 202 for batches of bills immediately.
The time until the bill finally gets closed isn't predictable. It depends on
- total number of bills to be closed (triggered either manually or automatically)
- availability of dependent micro services used for validations
- overall resource situation

If you depend on the final closing state you could check the bill status using GET /bills/identifier with an dynamic interval, e.g. first check status after 1 sec, then second check after 10 sec, then third check after 30 sec and so on.

I hope this information helps you.

Kind regards
Ralf

entry.title: Re: Notification for work order Clean Up
entry.content:
Hi M, Thank you for answering.

but what if the user did not tick that - does that mean notifications will not be closed while the work order remains in closed status?

I think there is a pile of notifications that are still open so I am looking for something where we can do a clean up to auto close notifications with closed work orders... like is there a background job for this?

entry.title: Re: ATC - How to Implement AUTHORITY-CHECK
entry.content:
Hi,
there is a check for missing AUTHORITY CHECK statements in certain contexts as part of the Code Vulnerability Analyzer (CVA) checks. See this blog: https://community.sap.com/t5/application-development-and-automation-blog-posts/code-vulnerability-analyzer-checks/ba-p/13339467

Is this what you we're looking for?

Regards,
Laura

entry.title: Re: Concat two dimension properties and pass it to booked...

entry.title: Re: SAP Workzone Local Entry Point plan availability
entry.content: Yes, the "local-entry-point" plan is now available in our Pay-As-You-Go account.

entry.title: How to Generate a Dynamic URL for Approval Task in SAP Business Process Automation (SAP BPA)
entry.content:
Hello Community,

I'm working on a workflow using SAP Build Process Automation and need help with generating a dynamic task approval link that can be sent via email notification to the approver.

Objective:

  • Generate a dynamic URL for each task instance.

  • Send the URL via email template or script task.

  • Clicking the link should open the approval task directly in My Inbox or SAP Build Work Zone / Launchpad.

Thanks in advance,
Parshuram

entry.title: Re: Automatic Absence Creation for Late coming/Early leaving in SuccessFactors Time Tracking
entry.content:
Hi @sharmama,

I am facing the same error 

"The result for time type group SG_CLT_DWT / (not valuated) is not calculated based on any time valuation assigned to your time recording profile. However, this group is used as input group, threshold group, comparison group or deduction group by a time valuation in your profile. Please have an administrator review your time recording profile and assign another time valuation that calculates time type group SG_CLT_DWT / (not valuated)."

Were you able to solve this?

entry.title: User does not have sufficient privileges in Build Apps
entry.content:
Hi

I have created a process automation like a bellow example 
https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/spa-academy-salesorder.html

here it is working fine, and workflow is triggering while creating the instance from Process and Workflow>> Start New instance and Close. 

But when i created the Build App for the API like bellow:

https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/build-apps-workflow-trigger.html

But here it is giving error at the time for workflow instance 

"responseText": "{\"error\":{\"code\":\"bpm.workflowruntime.rest.user.not.sufficient.privileges\",\"message\":\"User does not have sufficient privileges.\"}}"

I have assign the role also, But still giving error. Can any one help me please....

entry.title: Re: Impact convertion ECC to S/4HANA in SAP WM
entry.content: Hi @Dominik Tylcznski, Thank you very much, With this suggestion everything has now gone "green" in the "STOCKROOM_COMPLIANCE_CHECK" report. Now I have to check if it is possible to use "Room Management" on this client or if they really need EWM.

entry.title: Re: Python code error in dataflow
entry.content:
probably there is a typo related with tabs

def transform(data):
    specific_name = ['Male']
    df = data[data['gender'].isin(specific_name)]
    return df

entry.title: Re: Work zone differences and widgets
entry.content: The primary difference between SAP Build Work Zone and SAP SuccessFactors Work Zone lies in their focus and integration capabilities: SAP Build Work Zone is a part of SAP's Business Technology Platform, designed to provide a holistic interface for enterprise applications, offering customizable dashboards, integration with various SAP and non-SAP systems, and a more technical platform-centric approach. SAP SuccessFactors Work Zone, on the other hand, is specifically tailored for HR-related applications and processes, integrated deeply with SAP SuccessFactors HR solutions, focusing on enhancing employee experience, communication, and engagement within HR contexts.

entry.title: Re: Work zone differences and widgets
entry.content: See more info here: https://www.sap.com/products/hcm/workzone-hr.html https://www.sap.com/products/technology-platform/workzone.html

entry.title: Re: Python code error in dataflow
entry.content: Hi XaviPolo, The same code works in python notebooks or google colabs , but not in Datashpere . I hope that those "isin" functions not available yet in Datasphere.I can't write "import pandas " in datasphere.

entry.title: Unable to find checkbox Without Qty Posting under A/R Invoice form in SAP Business One 10.0
entry.content:
Dear All,

I am unable to find the checkbox Without Qty Posting under A/R Invoice form (in Item row in Contents tab) while choosing Transaction Type as GST Debit Memo in SAP Business One 10.0. Could someone suggest how to make it appear in Item row?

entry.title: FEC - How to reverse 2 postings with BKPF-BSTAT="O" (from RFOPENPOSTING_FR) ?
entry.content:
Hello,

we have an issue to run RFOPENPOSTING_FR in a company, as it has already been executed, generating 2 postings (with BKPF-BSTAT=O).

in order to run it again correctly, we would have first to reverse these 2 postings.

but it's not possible with FBRA.

how could it be possible to reverse them ?

 

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: Distribute amount to payment date based on invoice da...
entry.content:
Hi @Astra1562 

Please refer to the script below. 

MEMBERSET [d/Company_Code] = "A"
MEMBERSET [d/Date] = "2025-01-01" TO "2025-01-31"
MEMBERSET [d/Region]="EMEA"

INTEGER @Counter
INTEGER @PaymentDays

@PaymentDays = %Payment_Days%-1

FOR @Counter = 0 to @PaymentDays
	DATA([d/Measures]="InvoiceAmount",[d/PaymentDate]=NEXT(@Counter,"DAY",%Start_Payment%)) = RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures]="InvoiceAmount",[d/PaymentDate]="")
ENDFOR

I hope this is helpful to you, and if I have misunderstood anything, please feel free to reach out.

 

Moonjun

entry.title: Re: SAP SAC Data Action/Scripting - create new member and...

entry.title: How to Remove "One Time Deduction" from Action menu on People Profile - Employee Centra
entry.content: Manager can able to see employee's "One Time deduction" even after removing the permission: Logged in as Manager -> Under Manage My Team --> User Profile --> Actions --> Still can able to see One Time Deduction.

entry.title: Re: Creating Custom Work Distribution Category for Approval Process Definition
entry.content:
This is a few years after the initial comment but I thought I should add that I have tried this enhancement out for approval for expense report to change the approver based on an extension field value.

In the end the assignment to the UUID only worked when passing the Business Partner UUID for the approver in the following section
